complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down project initialization into: 1) Create root package.json with workspace configuration for monorepo, 2) Set up backend package with Express/TypeScript dependencies, 3) Initialize React frontend with Vite/TypeScript, 4) Create shared types package, 5) Configure Docker Compose with PostgreSQL/Redis services, 6) Set up environment configuration with dotenv, 7) Configure TypeScript paths and build scripts, 8) Create initial folder structure and README
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This is a greenfield project requiring complete setup of a full-stack TypeScript application with multiple services. Complexity comes from coordinating Docker, multiple TypeScript configs, monorepo setup, and ensuring all parts work together. No existing code means everything must be created from scratch.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Divide database work into: 1) Create migration setup with node-pg-migrate or similar tool, 2) Design and create Delegates table with enums and constraints, 3) Create Sessions table with garden state tracking, 4) Implement Queue table with position management, 5) Create SpeakingInstances table for history, 6) Add all foreign key relationships and indexes, 7) Create seed data and database initialization scripts
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Database schema is well-defined in PRD but requires careful implementation of enums, foreign keys, and constraints. The complexity includes setting up migrations, handling UUID generation, and ensuring referential integrity across four interconnected tables.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Structure API development as: 1) Set up Express server with TypeScript and middleware, 2) Create database connection pool and query builder setup, 3) Implement delegate CRUD endpoints with validation, 4) Add bulk import/export functionality for delegates, 5) Create session management endpoints, 6) Implement queue endpoints with business logic, 7) Add error handling and logging middleware, 8) Set up input validation with express-validator, 9) Configure CORS and security headers, 10) Write comprehensive API tests
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This requires building a complete REST API from scratch with multiple resource endpoints, complex validation, bulk operations, CSV handling, and proper error management. The high number of endpoints and need for robust validation increases complexity.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down queue logic into: 1) Create QueueService class architecture, 2) Implement first-time speaker priority algorithm, 3) Build on-deck position locking mechanism, 4) Create queue advancement logic with state transitions, 5) Add delegate validation and duplicate checking, 6) Implement Redis caching layer for queue state, 7) Build queue reordering functionality, 8) Handle concurrent queue operations with locks, 9) Create queue event emitters for WebSocket, 10) Add queue persistence and recovery, 11) Write extensive unit tests for all edge cases
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Queue management is the core business logic with complex priority rules, position locking, state management, and concurrent access concerns. Requires careful algorithm design, Redis integration, and extensive testing for edge cases.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Implement WebSocket system as: 1) Set up Socket.io server with TypeScript types, 2) Create admin and spectator namespaces with authentication, 3) Implement queue update events and broadcasting, 4) Add timer synchronization events, 5) Create demographic update streams, 6) Build room management for sessions, 7) Implement reconnection with state recovery, 8) Add connection pooling and scaling considerations, 9) Write integration tests for real-time events
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: WebSocket implementation requires namespace management, authentication, event design, and state synchronization. Complexity includes handling reconnections, ensuring message delivery, and managing multiple event types across different client types.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Build Timer System with Auto-start Logic
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Structure timer system as: 1) Create TimerService class with state management, 2) Implement countdown timer with pause/resume, 3) Add auto-start triggers on queue advancement, 4) Build auto-stop on speaker completion, 5) Create duration calculation and database updates, 6) Implement WebSocket timer tick events, 7) Add timer persistence for recovery, 8) Write tests for timer accuracy and edge cases
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Timer system requires precise timing, state management, automatic triggers, and WebSocket integration. Moderate complexity due to need for accuracy, persistence, and coordination with queue events.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Organize frontend development into: 1) Set up React with TypeScript and routing, 2) Create WebSocket context and connection management, 3) Build CurrentSpeaker component with styling, 4) Implement NextSpeaker and FollowingSpeaker components, 5) Create QueueGrid with 50-position display, 6) Build SpeakerCard with conditional styling, 7) Develop AdminView with tabbed interface, 8) Create DelegateManagement CRUD forms, 9) Build QueueControl with number input, 10) Implement responsive design system, 11) Add keyboard navigation and accessibility, 12) Create state management with Context/Zustand
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Frontend requires numerous components with complex state management, real-time updates, responsive design, and two distinct user interfaces. High complexity due to UI requirements, WebSocket integration, and accessibility needs.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down visualizations into: 1) Create BalanceIndicator component with SVG/Canvas, 2) Implement lever animation system, 3) Build real-time demographic calculations, 4) Create GardenVisualization component architecture, 5) Implement 33-state image loading and caching, 6) Build garden state calculation algorithm, 7) Add smooth transitions between garden states, 8) Create toggle controls for spectator view, 9) Optimize rendering performance for animations
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Visualizations require custom graphics, complex calculations, image management, and smooth animations. Complexity comes from real-time updates, performance optimization, and creating engaging visual feedback systems.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Structure analytics as: 1) Create AnalyticsService with calculation methods, 2) Build demographic participation queries, 3) Implement time distribution analysis, 4) Create real-time stats dashboard component, 5) Build CSV export with proper formatting, 6) Implement PDF generation with charts, 7) Add Redis caching for expensive queries, 8) Create scheduled aggregation jobs
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Analytics requires complex SQL queries, data aggregation, multiple export formats, and visualization. Moderate complexity with focus on performance optimization and accurate calculations.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Implement security in phases: 1) Set up bcrypt password hashing system, 2) Create JWT token generation and validation, 3) Implement refresh token mechanism, 4) Build dual authentication for admin/spectator, 5) Create audit log table and tracking, 6) Implement session timeout handling, 7) Add rate limiting middleware, 8) Set up data encryption for sensitive fields, 9) Configure HTTPS and SSL certificates, 10) Add CSRF protection and security headers
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Security implementation requires multiple layers of protection, careful handling of tokens, audit logging, and encryption. High complexity due to dual authentication system, various attack vectors to protect against, and compliance requirements.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Add Offline Support and Data Recovery
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Build offline capabilities as: 1) Create service worker with caching strategies, 2) Implement IndexedDB schema and sync logic, 3) Build offline queue for API calls, 4) Create conflict resolution algorithms, 5) Implement auto-save mechanism with intervals, 6) Build data recovery endpoints, 7) Create backup service with snapshots, 8) Add offline status indicators in UI, 9) Implement delta sync for efficiency, 10) Test offline/online transitions thoroughly
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Offline support requires service workers, complex sync logic, conflict resolution, and data integrity guarantees. High complexity due to state management across offline/online modes and handling edge cases.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Organize optimization and testing as: 1) Implement lazy loading and code splitting, 2) Add virtual scrolling for large lists, 3) Optimize WebSocket message batching, 4) Create database query optimizations, 5) Write comprehensive unit test suites, 6) Build integration tests for APIs, 7) Create E2E tests with Cypress, 8) Perform load testing with k6/Artillery, 9) Set up monitoring with Prometheus/Grafana
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Performance optimization and testing requires systematic approach across frontend, backend, and database layers. Complexity comes from achieving performance targets, comprehensive test coverage, and setting up monitoring infrastructure.
